mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real sales of autos and light trucks exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a deteriorating labor market that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side contraction that reduces aggregate output and corporate profits, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during post-shock recovery periods, with accuracy below 55% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month inflation readings exceeding 0.6% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger events, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling housing starts indicates a structural shift in demand composition that destabilizes financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5% signals an immediate supply chain bottleneck or demand shock that pressures equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.
